description:
`static/js/notifications.js:161` — `_renderEvent` builds an HTML string by concatenating `ev.body`, `ev.title`, and `ev.type` into innerHTML. Source trace per audit: these fields originate at `app/routes/dashboard.py:174,195,268` — built from char_name (EVE Online), skill_name/pname (SDE), and label (hardcoded `_STRUCTURE_ALERT_LABELS`).

Current attack surface: **essentially zero**. CCP disallows `<>"'&` in character names; SDE values and the hardcoded label map are server-controlled. But:
- The pattern is fragile — depends on third-party input restrictions remaining strict
- `_renderEvent` may be reused for less-trusted bodies later (corp join requests, mail subjects, anything user-typed)

Fix two options:
1. **Preferred:** rewrite `_renderEvent` to build DOM nodes via `document.createElement` + `textContent` rather than string concatenation
2. **Minimal:** add a tiny `esc()` helper (same pattern as the one in fitting_tool.html's implant code we added today) and wrap all interpolations

After fix, mark-attempted then re-run /audit to flip the finding.
title: Escape innerHTML interpolation in notifications.js (VVP-2026-021)
